rationale: The paper contributes a coherent and still historically important architecture for bidirectional pre-training and transfer-efficient NLP, with clear ablations and benchmark evidence. However, this repository is optimized around whitepaper processing, semantic indexing, and serving infrastructure, not full-scale transformer pre-training. Therefore, implementation should remain design-level and research-restricted until missing ML training and reproducibility infrastructure is added.
rejection_reasons: Reject immediate full production implementation: no in-repo BERT pretraining/fine-tuning/eval pipeline for GLUE+SQuAD+SWAG with deterministic manifests.
Reject deployment claims beyond research lane: compute, dataset licensing/access, and long-duration training controls are not currently implemented in this repo.
Reject immediate baseline replacement in active product logic: this repo's current mission path is workflow/orchestration and model-serving support, not replacement of production language stack.
recommendations: Proceed with this design as a documented, reproducibility-first reference implementation plan.
Create a dedicated training/evaluation service for NLP model replications before any rollout.
Add manifest-based dataset versioning (BooksCorpus/Wikipedia snapshot ids, tokenizer hash, random seeds, TPU/GPU config).
Require deterministic artifact checks and manual engineering approval before production promotion.
follow_up_actions: Draft and implement a concrete BERT training/eval blueprint if/when NLP platform scope is expanded.
Produce explicit reproducibility package (configs, seed logs, resource manifests) that can be compared against paper tables.
Add gate checks in whitepaper workflow for any future model-training artifacts and reject missing manifest fields.
